params.name: category
params.description: Categories that apps must have in order to be returned in the response

params.name: is_in_zap_template_with
params.description: If set, only return apps that are in a Zap Template with your app

params.name: page
params.description: Which page to return

params.name: per_page
params.description: Number of results to return per page

params.name: title_search
params.description: Case-insensitive parameter to limit the results to apps whose titles match

params.name: title_starts_with
params.description: Case-insensitive parameter to only return apps whose titles start with the parameter value in the response

items.docs: This endpoint returns a list of apps sorted popularity. See the List Apps guide to get started.

params.name: category
params.description:
Categories that apps must have in order to be returned in the response. 
The full list of valid categories can be retrieved using the `category` endpoint, detailed [here](https://platform.zapier.com/embed/partner-api#get-v1categories).

params.name: ids
params.description: Parameter to restrict the results to apps whose ID matches those in the provided comma-separated value. Cannot be combined with `category`.

params.name: limit
params.description: Used for paginating results. Specifies the maximum number of items to return per page. If this value is not set, it defaults to 10.

params.name: offset
params.description: Used for paginating results. Specifies the offset to use.

params.name: query
params.description: Parameter to limit the results to apps whose titles match the provided query.

items.docs:
This endpoint returns a list of apps sorted by popularity.

#### When using Client ID

Simply providing a client ID query parameter is sufficient. If an Authorization header is present, it will be used instead.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `zap` OAuth scope.

params.name: category
params.description: Filter apps by category slug.

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit the number of apps returned.

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of apps to skip before beginning to return results.

params.name: search
params.description: Case-insensitive search to filter apps by title.

items.docs: This endpoint returns a list of apps available for whitelabel connection. It is only for customers enrolled in the Zapier Whitelabel program. All others likely want the `Get Apps` endpoint instead.

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit the number of Zap categories returned.

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of Zap categories to skip over. The default value is 0, which is the offset of the first item.

items.docs: List of Zap categories

This endpoint returns the authenticated user information

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `profile` OAuth scope.

params.name: client_id
params.description: Your application Client ID.

params.name: redirect_uri
params.description: The page the user will be redirect to after OAuth flow.

params.name: response_type
params.description: Only OAuth response type `code` is supported

params.name: scope
params.description: Space (`%20`) separated values

params.name: sign_up_email
params.description: Email of the user signing up.

params.name: sign_up_first_name
params.description: First name of the user signing up.

params.name: sign_up_last_name
params.description: Last name of the user signing up.

items.docs: Create a new user and obtain an access token. See our Quick Account Creation guide to get started.

params.name: apps
params.description:
A comma separated list of Zapier Apps to match Zap templates against. Note:
- Your app will always be one of the apps in the template
- The list will return Zap Templates with all the provided apps, not a subset

params.name: limit
params.description: (Max: 100) Limit the number of Zap templates returned.

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of Zap templates to skip before beginning to return the Zap templates. The default value is 0, which is the offset of the first item.

items.docs: List popular Zap Templates using your app. See our List Zap Templates guide to get started.

params.name: get_params
params.description: If set, guarantees that the 'params' of any node belonging to your app will be present in the response

params.name: limit
params.description: (Max: 100) Limit the number of Zap templates returned.

params.name: offset
params.description: The number of Zap templates to skip before beginning to return the Zap templates. The default value is 0, which is the offset of the first item.

params.name: params__{{KEY}}
params.description: Filter for Zaps that contain the given parameter's {{KEY}} and the value of the query parameter. Keys are defined by your app on the [developer platform](https://zapier.com/platform/login).

items.docs:
This endpoint returns a list of Zaps for the authenticated Zapier user.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `zap` OAuth scope.

params.name: client_id
params.description: See our authentication documentation for how to find your client ID

items.docs: This endpoint returns a suggested Zap and pre-filled URL to Zapier from a given prompt.

params.name: expand
params.description: A comma separated list of Zap fields that should be expanded from ids to full objects in the response. Fields that may not be expanded will remain as ids.

params.name: include_shared
params.description: If true, all Zaps that are shared with the user, rather than only those owned by them, are returned. If the zap:account:all scope is not present this has no effect.

params.name: inputs
params.description: You may pass inputs[KEY]=VALUE1,VALUE2 to filter for Zaps that contain those settings. Keys are defined by your app on the developer platform.

params.name: limit
params.description: Used for paginating results. Specifies the maximum number of items to return per page. If this value is not set, it defaults to 10.

params.name: offset
params.description: Used for paginating results. Specifies the offset to use.

items.docs:
This endpoint returns a list of Zaps for the authenticated Zapier user.

The `expand` array can be used to expand selected fields into full objects in the response.  Inputs with keys can
also be passed to filter Zaps by certain criteria.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `zap`, `zap:all`, or `zap:account:all` OAuth scopes.

params.name: expand
params.description: A comma separated list of Zap fields that should be expanded from ids to full objects in the response. Fields that may not be expanded will remain as ids.

items.docs:
This URL creates a Zap based on the given steps and title.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `zap:write` OAuth scope.

Runs an action (step) in the third party API, using the provided authentication and inputs.

This endpoint is asynchronous, and the response will contain an Action Run ID. You can use the `/v2/action-runs/:id` endpoint to check the status of the run and retrieve the results.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `action:run` OAuth scope.

params.name: id
params.description: An Action Run ID.

items.docs:
Retrieves an Action Run.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `action:run` OAuth scope.

params.name: from_date
params.description: Filter Zap runs that occurred on or after this date. If not provided, the results default to Zap runs from the last 30 days.

params.name: limit
params.description: Used for paginating results. Specifies the maximum number of items to return per page. If this value is not set, it defaults to 10.

params.name: offset
params.description: Used for paginating results. Specifies the offset to use.

params.name: search
params.description: Performs a text search against the zap_title, data_in, and data_out fields, returning only zap runs that match the specified keywords.

params.name: statuses
params.description: Accepts one or more status values separated by comma, enabling the filtering of zap runs based on the specified status or statuses provided.

params.name: to_date
params.description: Filter Zap runs that occurred before this date.

params.name: zap_id
params.description: Find Zap runs for the specified Zap ID.

items.docs:
This endpoint returns runs for the specified Zaps and provides basic yet essential details about their execution. As the initial version, it serves foundational information, with plans for continuous enhancement to expand its capabilities and improve data output over time.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `zap:runs` OAuth scope.

params.name: action_type
params.description: The type of Action to filter for. Defaults to returning all actions regardless of type.

params.name: app
params.description: A canonical App ID, as provided by the `/apps` endpoint.

items.docs:
Fetch the available actions for the provided App. It's typical to filter by type so that only actions that make sense for a particular step are shown. Action IDs may not be reused, see our documentation for how to hardcode a particular action.

#### When using Client ID

Simply providing a client ID query parameter is sufficient. If an Authorization header is present, it will be used instead.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `zap` OAuth scope.

params.name: action_id
params.description: An Action ID, as provided by the `/actions` endpoint.

items.docs:
Get the Input Fields for a particular Action, using the provided authentication and inputs. See the fields and fieldsets guide for more information.
See [our docs](https://docs.zapier.com/powered-by-zapier/zap-creation/fields-and-fieldsets#input-fields) for more information.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `zap:write` OAuth scope.

params.name: action_id
params.description: An Action ID, as provided by the `/actions` endpoint.

params.name: input_id
params.description: An Input Field ID, as provided by the `/inputs` endpoint.

params.name: page
params.description: The page of choices to return, defaults to the first

items.docs:
Get the possible values for a `SELECT` Input Field.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `zap` OAuth scope.

params.name: action_id
params.description: An Action ID, as provided by the `/actions` endpoint.

items.docs:
Get the Output Fields for a particular Action, using the provided authentication and inputs.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `zap:write` OAuth scope.

params.name: action_id
params.description: An Action ID, as provided by the `/actions` endpoint.

items.docs:
Tests the action (step) in the third party api, using the provided authentication and inputs.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `zap:write` OAuth scope.

params.name: app
params.description: A canonical App ID, as provided by the `/apps` endpoint.

params.name: limit
params.description: Used for paginating results. Specifies the maximum number of items to return per page. If this value is not set, it defaults to 10.

params.name: offset
params.description: Used for paginating results. Specifies the offset to use.

items.docs:
Fetch the available Authentications for the provided App. This will only return Authentications that are owned by the user and not those that are shared with them, since it's not possible to create Zaps with Authentications you don't own.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `connection:read` OAuth scope.

Creates a new Authentication for the provided App. See our Adding an Authentication guide to get started.

#### When using OAuth

This endpoint requires the `connection:write` OAuth scope.
